metadata.title: 4th Pass Debug Factorial — GCP SkyPilot with SWAG + Calibration
metadata.priority: P0 — validates full factorial on cloud GPU before production spend
metadata.estimated-cost: ~$6-10 GCP L4 spot (24 trainable + 2 zero-shot, 2 epochs)

plan.user-prompt-verbatim: I think any further training after 2 epochs for 5 more epochs would improve the results so would not make too much of the numbers, update the report accordingly and commit and push, and then we can next create a 4th pass plan then finally for the Debug run on Skypilot+GCP as we did in /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment.xml and now create the xml plan to /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment-4th-pass.xml and then plan how to run this /factorial-monitor Skill, and the .sh should actually stay more or less the same? Obviously now SWAG is used before the old erroneously named SWA that was used as name for checkpoint averaging. Ask A LOT OF QUESTIONS in the interactive format when designing this plan so that we are 100% that we are aligned before burning the GCP credits for this debug factorial! As in the full factorial design, but only for 2 epochs (both training and SWAG), one fold and with half of the subjects. Otherwise the run should be EXACTLY as the real "publication-gate run" that we still have not completed. And that would obviously come after this run if we have everything running ok! Remember that in the last Skypilot+GCP run (/home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report.md), only Dynunet actually worked and we have no idea if SAM3, vesselfm and the mamba network will work! So put extra work when planning this .XML so that we can get the models running, and now we have the specific test suite also developed that should be run on the machine that it responsible for all the heavy GPU tasks (L4 now) which we should test once before running all the cells of the factorial design, and make sure that it characterizes the instance, and then checks that all the models actually train? We should have test suite at least implemented to catch all those issues before /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report.md without actually start running the real experiments. Should we even design a new Prefect Flow for this? Flow that basically has sub-flows: 1) something that can monitor the new configs where L4.yaml is now and if we add there a new .yaml, this Prefect Flow is then triggered, 2) one sub-flow that would always run once without much hard-coding, and it should adaptively as well figure from the .yaml used to trigger Train Flow which models are being tested and which model tests should run! E.g. if we run a factorial design with SAM3 and dynunet, no need to run the tests for vesselfm or mamba this time. If you think that this new Prefect Flow design and implementation is too massive, we can defer it? But either way create a P1 issue on this Prefect Flow for Instance testing. Open a PR for current branch and automerge it first after running the full prod suite first with no errors found, and then let's designing these new tasks to test/debug-factorial-4th-pass!

design-questions.question:
The 1st pass used "SWA" (which was actually checkpoint averaging). Now we have real SWAG (Maddox 2019). In the 4th pass factorial:
(a) Should SWAG be a POST-TRAINING plugin that runs after each training job completes (i.e., train 2 epochs → then SWAG 2 more epochs on same GPU)? (b) Or should SWAG be a separate SkyPilot job that runs AFTER all training jobs complete (like the post-training flow in the 1st pass plan)?
Option (a) means each job takes ~2× longer but no separate orchestration. Option (b) keeps training jobs fast but adds a second wave of SkyPilot jobs.
My recommendation: (a) — integrate SWAG into the training job. The SWAG plugin resumes training on the same checkpoint, same GPU, same data. Launching a separate job wastes setup time (Docker pull, DVC pull, model load).

The factorial design currently has: model × loss × aux_calib. With SWAG, we add a post_training factor: {none, swag, checkpoint_averaging}.
Should the 4th pass factorial be: 4 models × 3 losses × 2 aux_calib × 3 post_training = 72 conditions (+ 2 zero-shot)? Or keep it at: 4 models × 3 losses × 2 aux_calib × 2 post_training = 48 conditions (+ 2 zero-shot)? (dropping checkpoint_averaging since 2nd pass showed it's negligible) Or even simpler for debug: 4 models × 3 losses × 2 aux_calib = 24 training jobs, then apply SWAG to ALL? (SWAG applied post-hoc to every checkpoint, no extra training jobs)
My recommendation: 24 training jobs + SWAG applied post-hoc to all. This matches the 1st pass design (same number of training jobs) but replaces checkpoint averaging with SWAG. Keeps debug cost at ~$6-10.

You mentioned a Prefect Flow for instance testing. The idea is: 1. When a new GPU instance starts, run model-specific tests BEFORE training 2. Adaptively select which tests to run based on the factorial design
This would have caught Glitches #9 (LoRA Conv2d), #10 (mamba-ssm), and #12 (zero-shot max_epochs=0) from the 1st pass before burning GPU time.
Design options: (a) Full Prefect sub-flow with config-file monitoring — ambitious, reusable (b) Simple Python script that runs in SkyPilot setup phase — pragmatic, fast (c) Deferred to P1 issue — just run existing tests/gpu_instance/ manually first
Given this is blocking the 4th pass, I recommend (b) for now + (a) as P1 issue. A simple script in the SkyPilot YAML setup phase that: - Detects which models are in the factorial design (from env vars) - Runs model-specific smoke tests (import, build, 1-batch forward pass) - Fails fast if any model can't even load
This catches 90% of issues without a full Prefect flow.

The 1st pass had these unresolved model failures:
| Model | Glitch | Status | What needs fixing | |-------|--------|--------|-------------------| | sam3_topolora | #9: LoRA on Conv2d | OPEN | LoRA targeting must skip Conv2d | | mambavesselnet | #10: mamba-ssm not compiled | OPEN | Docker image needs INSTALL_MAMBA=1 | | sam3_vanilla (zero-shot) | #12: max_epochs=0 | OPEN | Pydantic ge=1 constraint | | vesselfm (zero-shot) | #12: max_epochs=0 | OPEN | Same Pydantic constraint |
Should we: (a) Fix ALL four before the 4th pass (adds 1-2 days of work) (b) Fix sam3_topolora + zero-shot (likely quick), defer mamba-ssm (Docker rebuild) (c) Run 4th pass with only dynunet + sam3_hybrid (known working), defer rest
My recommendation: (b) — fix LoRA targeting and zero-shot validation, but defer mamba-ssm Docker compilation to a separate issue. That gives us 3/4 models working (dynunet, sam3_hybrid, sam3_topolora) + 2 zero-shot baselines.

Glitch #8 from the 1st pass: ALL 11 succeeded training jobs had LOST checkpoints because MLflow Cloud Run was configured with --serve-artifacts (proxying), which doesn't persist to GCS.
Is this fixed? The fix was: - MLflow Cloud Run with --no-serve-artifacts - Direct GCS artifact storage (gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts) - Or: SkyPilot file_mounts to sync checkpoints to GCS directly
If NOT fixed, this is still a P0 blocker for the 4th pass.

The 4th pass needs a Docker image with: - All SWAG plugin code (from this branch) - All calibration metrics code - Fixed LoRA targeting (if Q4 answer includes sam3_topolora) - Optionally: mamba-ssm compiled (if Q4 answer includes mambavesselnet)
This means we need to: 1. Merge this branch to main 2. Rebuild Docker base image from main 3. Push to GAR
Should we do this as part of the 4th pass prep, or is there a faster path?

The debug_factorial.yaml doesn't currently reference calibration metrics. Should we add calibration_metrics config to the factorial YAML, or are they automatically computed by the training loop (SegmentationMetrics) and analysis flow (UnifiedEvaluationRunner)?
From the 3rd pass: Tier 1 calibration metrics (ECE, Brier, NLL) are computed in the training validation loop. Tier 2 (ACE, BA-ECE) are computed in the analysis flow. No YAML changes needed for metrics.
But: should the BiostatisticsConfig be updated to include calibration metrics as ANOVA response variables for the 4th pass?

The .sh script should "stay more or less the same" as you said. The only changes I see needed are:
(a) Add SWAG_EPOCHS env var to SkyPilot launch (if SWAG runs in-job per Q1) (b) Add SWAG_LR, SWAG_MAX_RANK env vars (c) Update job naming: include "swag" in condition name if applicable
Or should the .sh stay IDENTICAL and SWAG be handled entirely by the post-training flow (downstream, not in training jobs)?

phase.name: Launch Training Conditions
phase.description: PLACEHOLDER — depends on Q1, Q2, Q8 answers

phase.name: Post-Training (SWAG)
phase.description: PLACEHOLDER — depends on Q1, Q2 answers
